$comment: Story playbook — the per-story sequence of BMAD actions executed by the 'Develop Epic' n8n loop. This file is the source of truth; the n8n workflow only interprets it. scripts/pipeline/apply-amendments.mjs may update the prompt of any step, 'core' or 'learned', but may only retire (disable) steps with origin 'learned' — retiring a 'core' step stays human-only. Adjacent steps sharing a 'stage' value are order-independent (carried over from the gen-1 workflow's fan-out groups); execution is still strictly sequential in array order — the stage field only preserves which steps could run in parallel if that is ever built.

steps.label: Create story
steps.prompt: run Creation mode. When a decision arises, apply _bmad-output/decision-policy.md: if a rule covers it, decide, record the decision and rule ID in the story file, and continue. HALT only for decisions the policy does not cover.

steps.label: Validate story (1st pass)
steps.prompt: validate story. When a decision arises, apply _bmad-output/decision-policy.md: if a rule covers it, decide, record the decision and rule ID in the story file, and continue. HALT only for decisions the policy does not cover.

steps.label: Validate story (2nd pass)
steps.prompt: validate story. When a decision arises, apply _bmad-output/decision-policy.md: if a rule covers it, decide, record the decision and rule ID in the story file, and continue. HALT only for decisions the policy does not cover.

steps.label: Implement story
steps.prompt: develop story autonomously, completing every task listed in the story spec before finishing. As each task makes a skipped test pass, remove its test.skip()/it.skip() or another equivalent marker — a skipped test provides zero signal. The story is not complete while skipped tests remain for tasks that are done. When a decision arises, apply _bmad-output/decision-policy.md: if a rule covers it, decide, record the decision and rule ID in the story file, and continue. HALT only for decisions the policy does not cover.

steps.label: Unit tests
steps.prompt: Autonomously evaluate (Validate) if the Story is sufficiently covered. If needed, expand coverage (Create/Resume). Treat any skipped test (test.skip, it.skip, describe.skip, xtest, test.fixme, it.todo, or framework equivalent) as a coverage failure — a skipped test provides zero coverage. For each skipped test: un-skip and run it. If it passes, keep it un-skipped. If it fails, either fix the implementation so it passes or remove the test entirely, recording the reason in the story file. The only acceptable resolved state is zero skipped tests. When a decision arises, apply _bmad-output/decision-policy.md: if a rule covers it, decide, record the decision and rule ID in the story file, and continue. HALT only for decisions the policy does not cover.

steps.label: Code review
steps.prompt: Review the implementation of the story below. Use the story file in {implementation_artifacts} matching this story's key (pattern `<epic>-<story>-*.md`) as the explicit spec file (Tier 1) so the review target and diff baseline (its frontmatter `baseline_commit`) resolve without asking; if no `baseline_commit` is present, review all uncommitted changes instead of asking which target. If the diff exceeds the chunking threshold, take the first chunk automatically and note remaining groups for a follow-up run — do not wait for confirmation. Skip the pre-review checkpoint confirmation and proceed straight to the review. For decision-needed findings, apply _bmad-output/decision-policy.md: if a rule covers it, decide, record the decision and rule ID in the story file, and continue — if the resolution is to defer, write your own one-line reason instead of asking. HALT only for decisions the policy does not cover. Once findings are triaged, choose 'apply every patch' (fix all patch findings now, no per-finding confirmation), then end the workflow rather than offering the next-steps menu.

steps.label: Test review
steps.prompt: Review tests. Treat any skipped test related to the story (test.skip, it.skip or another equivalent) as a test-quality failure — flag it for un-skipping or removal with a recorded reason.

steps.label: NFR review
steps.prompt: Run NFR audit for the story. Identify and apply NFR-specific patches only (performance, security, reliability, scalability — e.g., select projection, take limits, timing tests, security headers). Do not implement features, refactor unrelated code, create database migrations, or fix test quality issues — those belong in dev or test-review steps. Document deferred findings in the story's review section.

steps.label: Update project context
steps.prompt: Review the story artifacts and the code implemented for it. Identify any new, repeatable patterns or conventions that emerged and aren't already captured in project-context.md. If none exist, do nothing. Otherwise, append them to project-context.md — keep entries lean, document only what's unobvious and repeatable. This is routine work, its outcome size depends on the uniqueness of the story.
